digest.top_line: DBCG IMN2: 15yr OS 65.0% vs 60.8% with IMNI (HR 0.85, p=0.0016); internal mammary nodal RT benefit holds under modern systemic therapy.
digest.tldr: Regional nodal RT carried the day. Breast IMN2 (nationwide cohort, N=4541) keeps internal-mammary irradiation's OS edge alive under modern systemic Rx, while prostate PEACE V-STORM is the first randomised ENRT-vs-MDT read (4-yr MFS 76% vs 63%, p=0·063, phase 2). Skin adds EORTC low-dose RT dosing for indolent cutaneous lymphomas.

sites.intro: EORTC consensus on RT dose per cutaneous lymphoma entity, though no RCT fixes a standard.

studies.name: EORTC Cutaneous Lymphoma RT Recommendations
studies.tldr: Low-dose RT (8-12 Gy) recommended for indolent cutaneous lymphomas; MF 1-yr local control ≥92%, but no RCT defines a standard dose per entity.

details.text: 🔍 RT dose recommendations by cutaneous lymphoma entity
⚠️ 4 Gy (1-2 fx) gave inferior local control vs 8-12 Gy
🔗 Folliculotropic MF: RT more effective than other modalities (Dutch registry, 203 cases, retrospective)

details: 🔍 Sézary syndrome: TSEBT controversial, rarely achieves long-term remission
💊 TSEBT in SS also lowers circulating Sézary cell counts (systemic effect)

details.text: 💊 12 Gy TSEBT + mechlorethamine gel maintenance reported with encouraging results

details: ⚠️ No completed RCTs defining standard dose per entity; prospective trial urgently needed
studies.significance: The actionable read is the dose floor: 4 Gy (1-2 fx) underperformed vs 8-12 Gy, so 8-12 Gy stays the low-dose local standard for MF, 24 Gy reserved for large/refractory lesions or pre-autoSCT remission. Low-dose TSEBT trades durability for repeatability and lower toxicity, moving the de-escalation decision in indolent CTCL.
studies.monday_clinic: In indolent cutaneous lymphoma (MF plaque/tumour, pcALCL, cutaneous B-cell), this supports low-dose RT (8-12 Gy) as effective skin-directed treatment; it does not set a dose for aggressive CTCL variants, where low-dose RT has a limited role.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: EORTC cutaneous lymphoma tumour group **expert-opinion / consensus** recommendations, synthesizing retrospective case series and limited prospective registries. Proposes a **treatment algorithm** to standardize radiation dose across EORTC centres.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Modalities span **electrons, photons, kilovoltage X-ray, brachytherapy**. Low-dose regimens favored for low toxicity and **repeatability** on relapse; higher-dose TSEBT reserved for remission induction before autologous SCT.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents indolent primary cutaneous lymphomas (MF, pcALCL, CD4+ small/medium T-cell LPD, cutaneous B-cell). Does not represent aggressive CTCL variants (SPTCL, gamma/delta, CD8+ epidermotropic, NK/T-cell), where low-dose RT has a limited role.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **No completed RCTs** define the standard dose per entity; recommendations rest on retrospective series and expert consensus. Authors call a prospective trial **urgently needed**.

open_questions: Optimal radiation dose per cutaneous lymphoma entity
Durability of low-dose TSEBT vs higher-dose regimens
TSEBT combined with immunotherapy in Sézary syndrome

related_trials.brief_title: Low Dose Radiotherapy in Primary Indolent Cutaneous B-cell Lymphoma
related_trials.brief_summary: Primary indolent cutaneous B cell lymphomas (PCBCL) are rare: although data on outcomes and treatment are limited, traditionally they have been treated with radiation doses in excess of 24 Gy. Recently, some trials that patients with primary cutaneous indolent lymphoma managed with very low dose (4 Gy) RT (LDRT) have shown that high response rates and durable remission can be achieved; unfortunately, given the retrospective nature of these studies, the role of LDRT in indolent PMCL remains…

* Age greater than or equal to 18 years
* Histological diagnosis of indolent B-cell lymphoma (follicular, marginal, NOS)
* Primary cutaneous location
* Early stage (I-II)
* First diagnosis or recurrence
* Low-dose radiotherapy treatment (4 Gy/2 sessions)

related_trials.relevance_phrase: 4 Gy low-dose RT in cutaneous B-cell lymphoma

related_trials.brief_title: Ultra Low Dose Radiation Therapy in Treating Patients With Mycosis Fungoides
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial studies how well ultra low dose radiation therapy works in treating patients with mycosis fungoides. Radiation therapy uses high energy x-rays to kill tumor cells and shrink tumors. Giving ultra low doses of radiation may help control the disease and reduce side effects compared to treatment with higher doses.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

* Patients with pathologically confirmed MF with cutaneous involvement.

  * If a patient has a prior pathological diagnosis of MF and is clinically diagnosed with a new lesion,…

related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 2 ultra-low-dose RT vs higher-dose in MF

related_trials.brief_title: TOtal Skin Electron Beam Therapy (Low-dose) for Tumor Clone Eradication in Early-stage Mycosis Fungoides
related_trials.brief_summary:
Primary cutaneous T-cell lymphomas are a group of peripheral T-cell lymphomas that primarily involve the skin. Mycosis fungoides (MF) is the most frequent subtype. Most patients with early-stage MF (i.e., patches and plaques of the skin without extracutaneous involvement) have a good prognosis but a subset of patients progress to incurable advanced-stage disease with an overall survival (OS) less than 5 years and an impaired quality of life.

We have recently identified the tumor clone…

Inclusion Criteria:

* Age ≥ 18 years
* Histopathologically confirmed diagnosis of International Society for Cutaneous Lymphomas (ISCL) / European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer (EORTC) mycosis fungoides stage IB or IIA

Exclusion Criteria:

* Poor performance status: WHO…

related_trials.brief_title: Mogamulizumab + Low-Dose Total Skin Electron Beam Tx in Mycosis Fungoides & Sézary Syndrome
related_trials.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the efficacy of the combination of LD-TSEBT and mogamulizumab in patients with MF and SS. And to evaluate the secondary measures of clinical benefit of the combination therapy and to evaluate the safety and tolerability of the combination in patients with MF and SS.

sites.intro: DBCG IMN2 revives the internal-mammary nodal RT question for the modern-therapy era.

studies.name: DBCG IMN2
studies.tldr: 15yr OS 65.0% vs 60.8% with IMNI, adjusted HR 0.85 (0.76-0.94); benefit persists under modern systemic therapy and 3D RT.
details: 🔍 Prospective nationwide population-based cohort, N=4541, 6 RT centres; right-sided tumours → IMNI, left-sided → no IMNI. Median f/u 13.7yr.

details.text: 📊 IMNI vs no IMNI, adjusted HRs (Cox)

details: 📊 IMNI benefit maintained in 1-3 positive-node pts; no subgroup identified for IMNI omission
💊 Modern systemic backbone (taxanes, trastuzumab, aromatase inhibitors), the era thought to erase residual IMNI gain
📊 15yr cardiac death (ischemic/valvular) 0.2% right/IMNI vs 0.7% left; no excess cardiac mortality from IMNI
⚠️ Non-randomized: IMNI assigned by tumour laterality, not randomization; residual confounding possible
⚠️ Excludes neoadjuvant-treated pts; result doesn't transfer to the growing NAC population

verdict.rationale: Large prospective laterality-allocated cohort, not randomized; confirms IMN1 + EBCTCG that IMNI benefit persists with modern systemic therapy. Non-random design caps it below practice-changing.
verdict.audience: Node-positive breast cancer, incl. 1-3 positive nodes; no neoadjuvant therapy

studies.significance: The 1-3 positive-node subgroup is the RT read: IMNI benefit held at the lowest nodal burden, no subgroup found for omission, moving elective IMN coverage where guidelines diverge. Reassuring on toxicity too: 15yr ischemic/valvular cardiac death 0.2% (right/IMNI) vs 0.7% (left) under 3D planning.
studies.monday_clinic: In node-positive breast cancer treated with upfront surgery then modern systemic therapy, especially 1-3 positive nodes, this supports including internal mammary nodes in the RT target; it does not extend to neoadjuvant-treated pts, who were excluded.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Prospective nationwide population-based cohort**, N=4541, 6 RT centres, treated 2007-14. IMNI assigned by tumour laterality: **right-sided → IMNI, left-sided → no IMNI**. Median follow-up **13.7 yr**.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Node-positive breast cancer, including the **1-3 positive-node** low-burden group. Excluded prior malignancy, bilateral cancer, **neoadjuvant systemic therapy**, pre-RT recurrence, non-standard RT.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Modern systemic backbone: **taxane chemotherapy, trastuzumab, aromatase inhibitors**, the era hypothesized to shrink absolute IMNI gain.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **3D-based RT**. IMNI delivered to right-sided tumours only; laterality allocation balances cardiac dose. Dose/fractionation not reported in source.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: overall survival.** Secondary: breast cancer mortality, distant metastasis.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: IMNI improved all three endpoints (see table). Absolute **15yr OS gain 4.2%** (65.0% vs 60.8%).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Confirms **DBCG IMN1** (+4.7% abs OS at 14.8 yr) and the **EBCTCG** meta-analysis (+3% abs 15yr survival, regional node RT); contradicts the negative Korean **KROG 06-08** in the modern-therapy era.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents node-positive breast cancer, including 1-3 positive nodes, treated with upfront surgery and modern systemic therapy. Does not represent neoadjuvant-treated patients, who were excluded.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Non-randomized: IMNI assigned by tumour laterality rather than randomization, so residual confounding is possible despite balanced baseline characteristics.

open_questions: Randomized confirmation of IMNI benefit in 1-3 node patients
IMNI value in neoadjuvant-treated patients (excluded here)
IMNI cardiac safety with modern breath-hold or proton planning

related_trials.brief_title: Robustness Evaluation of Deep Inspiration Breath-Hold (DIBH) Plans in Internal Mammary Irradiation
related_trials.brief_summary: This study is an investigator-initiated, single-arm, single-center, prospective, observational study. The hypothesis is that during the implementation of deep inspiration breath-hold (DIBH) radiotherapy plans in postoperative breast cancer patients receiving internal mammary irradiation, the actual target dose coverage and organ-at-risk (OARs) dose parameters remain within clinically acceptable ranges.

4. Have received breast-conserving surgery or mastectomy with axillary…

related_trials.brief_title: Proton vs Photon IMRT Toxicity in Breast Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This study aims to compare the differences in acute and long-term toxicities between intensity-modulated proton therapy (IMPT) and intensity-modulated photon radiotherapy (IMRT/VMAT) in postoperative breast cancer patients, with a focus on evaluating their impact on critical organs, including the heart, lungs, skin, esophagus, thyroid, and lymphatic tissues. Eligible patients will be followed for at least one year to assess the incidence and severity of both acute and late toxicities, as well…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

4. For invasive carcinoma, sentinel lymph node biopsy,…

related_trials.relevance_phrase: IMPT vs IMRT toxicity, heart among key OARs

sites.intro: PEACE V-STORM pits elective nodal RT against metastasis-directed therapy for pelvic nodal oligorecurrence.

studies.name: PEACE V-STORM
studies.tldr: 4-yr MFS 76% vs 63% favoring ENRT over MDT for pelvic nodal oligorecurrence, HR 0·62 (80% CI 0·44-0·86, p=0·063).
details: 🔍 Phase 2 open-label RCT, 196 randomised (MDT 99, ENRT 97) across 21 hospitals, 6 countries
🔍 Population: PET-detected pelvic nodal oligorecurrence (≤5 nodes), post radical local Rx, WHO PS 0-1
🔍 ENRT: 45 Gy/25fx whole pelvis + SIB 65 Gy to PET-positive nodes (or salvage LND), 6mo ADT
🔍 MDT: SBRT 30 Gy/3fx every other day (or salvage LND), 6mo ADT

details.text: ⚠️ Grade 3 AEs by arm (numerically higher with ENRT)

details: 🔗 First randomised ENRT-vs-MDT head-to-head; prior oligomet RCTs (STOMP, ORIOLE) tested MDT vs observation, not elective nodal RT
⚠️ 1° EP p=0·063 on an 80% CI phase 2 screening design; authors frame ENRT as potential SOC awaiting phase 3

verdict.rationale: First randomised ENRT-vs-MDT comparison; ENRT's MFS edge diverges from MDT-favouring practice. Phase 2 screening design, p=0·063, awaits phase 3.
verdict.audience: Pelvic nodal oligorecurrent prostate (≤5 nodes), post radical local Rx, PS 0-1

studies.significance: The RT read: whole-pelvis ENRT (45 Gy/25fx + SIB 65 Gy) beat node-only MDT on 4-yr MFS (76% vs 63%, HR 0·62), consistent with occult pelvic nodal disease driving the failures MDT leaves untreated. Moves the elective-nodal-coverage decision in ≤5-node pelvic recurrence.
studies.monday_clinic: In men with PET-detected pelvic nodal-only oligorecurrence (≤5 nodes) after radical local treatment, this favors whole-pelvis ENRT over node-only MDT; it does not extend to extrapelvic M1, bone, or visceral oligometastatic recurrence.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 2, open-label, randomised (1:1) **screening** trial, 21 hospitals in 6 countries. **196 randomised** (MDT 99, ENRT 97), 190 evaluable, modified ITT. Median follow-up **50 mo** (IQR 42-58).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Men with **PET-detected pelvic nodal oligorecurrence** (≤5 nodes) after radical local prostate treatment; WHO PS 0-1, histologically confirmed adenocarcinoma. All male.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **ENRT:** 45 Gy/25fx whole pelvis + **SIB 65 Gy** to PET-positive nodes (or salvage LND). **MDT:** SBRT **30 Gy/3fx** every other day (or salvage LND). Both + **6 mo ADT**. Stratified by tracer (choline vs PSMA) and MDT type.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary:** metastasis-free survival (any M1 on PET or death), modified ITT. Reported with **80% CIs**, a phase 2 screening threshold, not the conventional 95%.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Grade 3 events low in both arms and numerically higher with ENRT (urinary incontinence, diarrhoea). **No treatment-related deaths.**

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: First randomised **ENRT-vs-MDT** comparison for nodal oligorecurrence. Prior oligomet RCTs (**STOMP**, **ORIOLE**) tested MDT vs observation, not elective nodal RT, so this adds the missing head-to-head.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents PET-detected pelvic nodal-only oligorecurrent prostate cancer (≤5 nodes) after radical local treatment. Does not represent extrapelvic M1, bone, or visceral oligometastatic recurrence.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Open-label**; primary endpoint **p=0·063** did not clear conventional significance and rests on a phase 2 screening design. Authors position ENRT as a **potential** standard awaiting phase 3.

open_questions: Phase 3 confirmation of ENRT superiority over MDT
Whether the MFS benefit translates to overall survival
Long-term GU and GI toxicity of whole-pelvis ENRT with SIB

related_trials.brief_title: Elective Adaptive Radiation With SBRT for Improved DurabilitY of Response
related_trials.brief_summary: This prospective, single-arm feasibility study will include patients with prostate cancer that have controlled local disease diagnosed with oligorecurrent para-aortic nodal disease and/or common iliac nodal disease detected by PSMA PET or conventional CT/MRI imaging, and these patients will undergo CT-guided online adaptive SBRT to the tumor and elective SBRT to adjacent at-risk nodal regions.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Histologically confirmed prostate adenocarcinoma.
2. Oligorecurrent para-aortic +/- common iliac nodal disease planned for SBRT.
3. ≤10 involved para-aortic (defined as between L1/L2 interface and L4/L5 interface) +/- common iliac LN on PSMA PET imaging.
4. ECOG performance…
related_trials.answers_question: Phase 3 confirmation of ENRT superiority over MDT
related_trials.relevance_phrase: elective nodal SBRT added to MDT for nodal oligorecurrence
